  "text": "The Node.js CommonJS to ESM module loader migration surface implements a sophisticated two-stage module loading system:\n\n## Core Entry Point Detection\nThe entry point detection occurs in `lib/internal/modules/run_main.js`, which contains `executeUserEntryPoint`, `shouldUseESMLoader`, and `resolveMainPath`. These functions determine whether the main entry point should be loaded via ESM or CJS based on file extension (.mjs, .cjs), package.json type field, and CLI options. The `runEntryPointWithESMLoader` function initializes the ESM loader and executes user imports before loading the entry point module.\n\n## ESM Loader Architecture\nThe main ESM loader is implemented in `lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js`, which contains the `ModuleLoader` class (line 110) and `CustomizedModuleLoader` class (line 727). ModuleLoader serves as the base machinery for module loading, implementing the core load(), resolve(), and import() methods. The loader can be customized through hooks for custom resolution and loading behavior.\n\n## Module Resolution System\nResolution is handled in `lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js` with several key functions:\n- `moduleResolve` (line 899) - Main resolution entry point\n- `defaultResolve` (line 1011) - Default resolution implementation\n- `finalizeResolution` (line 228) - Normalizes and validates resolved URLs\n- `packageExportsResolve` (line 588) - Resolves exports from package.json\n- `packageImportsResolve` (line 695) - Resolves import maps\n- `packageResolve` (line 801) - Resolves package names to file paths\n- `resolveAsCommonJS` (line 941) - Fallback CJS resolution for interop\n\n## CJS to ESM Interop Bridge\nThe CJS loader in `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js` contains `finalizeEsmResolution` (line 1258), which is the ESM fallback mechanism. This function allows require() to seamlessly fall through to ESM resolution when appropriate, enabling mixed module ecosystems.\n\n## Module Format Translators\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js` file implements format-specific handlers:\n- `translators` Map (line 82) - Registry of format handlers\n- Format handlers: 'module' (ES modules), 'commonjs' (CJS), 'json' (JSON files), 'wasm' (WebAssembly), 'builtin' (native modules), and TypeScript variants\n- `loadCJSModule` (line 117) - Wraps CommonJS modules in ESM wrapper\n- `createCJSModuleWrap` (line 176) - Creates ModuleWrap objects for CJS modules\n\n## Module Job Pipeline\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js` implements the module lifecycle:\n- `ModuleJob` (line 72) - Main class tracking module loading and dependencies\n- `ModuleJobBase` (line 60) - Base class with common properties\n- `ModuleJobSync` (line 310) - Synchronous variant for require() from ESM\n- The `_link()` method recursively links all dependencies\n- The `run()` method evaluates the module via native ModuleWrap binding\n\n## Native C++ Binding\nThe `src/module_wrap.h` and `src/module_wrap.cc` files contain the `ModuleWrap` class (line 36 in header), which is a V8 Module binding that:\n- Wraps V8's native module implementation\n- Provides `link()` and `evaluate()` methods called by ModuleJob\n- Handles instantiation and evaluation with support for source mapping and debugging\n\n## Supporting Systems\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js` - Default load implementation with `defaultLoad` and `defaultLoadSync`\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/get_format.js` - Format detection via `defaultGetFormat` and MIME type matching\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js` - `Hooks` and `HooksProxy` classes for custom loader registration\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/utils.js` - `compileSourceTextModule`, `initializeESM`, `initializeHooks`\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/initialize_import_meta.js` - `initializeImportMeta` and import.meta.resolve support\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_map.js` - `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` for memoization\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/fetch_module.js` - Remote module fetching for http(s):// URLs\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/formats.js` - Format constants and detection helpers\n- `src/node_modules.h` - `BindingData` for module resolution internals\n\nThe architecture enables Node.js to transparently load both ESM and CJS modules, with automatic interop bridges allowing require() in CJS to load ESM and import() in ESM to load CJS, supported by a sophisticated caching and memoization layer."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Node.js CommonJS to ESM Module Loader Migration Surface

## Your Task

Find all JavaScript and C++ source files in nodejs/node that implement the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge. Identify: the ModuleLoader class (lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js), the CJS loader's ESM fallback (finalizeEsmResolution in lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js), the ESM translators for different formats, the ESM specifier resolver, the ModuleJob linking/evaluation pipeline, the entry point ESM detection (run_main.js), and the native ModuleWrap C++ binding.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/node--v22.13.0.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/node--v22.13.0` (nodejs/node)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find the ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge implementation in the Node.js repository. Let me start by exploring the workspace and locating the key files.

Perfect! I've successfully created the comprehensive analysis of the Node.js ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge. Here's what I've documented:

## Summary of Findings

I've identified all the key components of the Node.js module loading system:

### **Core Components Found:**

1. **ModuleLoader Class** - `lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js:110`
   - Base machinery for ESM module loading with resolve(), load(), and import() methods
   - CustomizedModuleLoader variant for hook-based customization

2. **CJS-ESM Interop** - `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:1258`
   - `finalizeEsmResolution` function providing ESM fallback for require()

3. **Module Resolution System** - `lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js`
   - `moduleResolve` - Main resolution entry point
   - `packageExportsResolve` - Package.json exports resolution
   - `resolveAsCommonJS` - CJS fallback resolver

4. **Format Translators** - `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js`
   - Handlers for ES modules, CommonJS, JSON, WebAssembly, and TypeScript formats
   - `loadCJSModule` and `createCJSModuleWrap` for CJS wrapping

5. **ModuleJob Pipeline** - `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js`
   - `ModuleJob` class for tracking module loading and linking dependencies
   - `_link()` method for recursive dependency resolution
   - `run()` method for module evaluation

6. **Entry Point Detection** - `lib/internal/modules/run_main.js`
   - `executeUserEntryPoint` - Main entry point handler
   - `shouldUseESMLoader` - Determines module type
   - `resolveMainPath` - Path resolution logic

7. **Native C++ Binding** - `src/module_wrap.h/cc`
   - `ModuleWrap` class wrapping V8's native module implementation
   - Link and evaluate methods for module instantiation

The answer has been written to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, symbols, execution chain, and detailed narrative explanation.

arguments.description: Find the main ESM and CJS loader files
Find run_main.js for ESM entry point detection
Find ModuleJob methods for linking and evaluation

  "text": "The Node.js CommonJS to ESM module loader migration surface implements a sophisticated two-stage module loading system:\n\n## Core Entry Point Detection\nThe entry point detection occurs in `lib/internal/modules/run_main.js`, which contains `executeUserEntryPoint`, `shouldUseESMLoader`, and `resolveMainPath`. These functions determine whether the main entry point should be loaded via ESM or CJS based on file extension (.mjs, .cjs), package.json type field, and CLI options. The `runEntryPointWithESMLoader` function initializes the ESM loader and executes user imports before loading the entry point module.\n\n## ESM Loader Architecture\nThe main ESM loader is implemented in `lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js`, which contains the `ModuleLoader` class (line 110) and `CustomizedModuleLoader` class (line 727). ModuleLoader serves as the base machinery for module loading, implementing the core load(), resolve(), and import() methods. The loader can be customized through hooks for custom resolution and loading behavior.\n\n## Module Resolution System\nResolution is handled in `lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js` with several key functions:\n- `moduleResolve` (line 899) - Main resolution entry point\n- `defaultResolve` (line 1011) - Default resolution implementation\n- `finalizeResolution` (line 228) - Normalizes and validates resolved URLs\n- `packageExportsResolve` (line 588) - Resolves exports from package.json\n- `packageImportsResolve` (line 695) - Resolves import maps\n- `packageResolve` (line 801) - Resolves package names to file paths\n- `resolveAsCommonJS` (line 941) - Fallback CJS resolution for interop\n\n## CJS to ESM Interop Bridge\nThe CJS loader in `lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js` contains `finalizeEsmResolution` (line 1258), which is the ESM fallback mechanism. This function allows require() to seamlessly fall through to ESM resolution when appropriate, enabling mixed module ecosystems.\n\n## Module Format Translators\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js` file implements format-specific handlers:\n- `translators` Map (line 82) - Registry of format handlers\n- Format handlers: 'module' (ES modules), 'commonjs' (CJS), 'json' (JSON files), 'wasm' (WebAssembly), 'builtin' (native modules), and TypeScript variants\n- `loadCJSModule` (line 117) - Wraps CommonJS modules in ESM wrapper\n- `createCJSModuleWrap` (line 176) - Creates ModuleWrap objects for CJS modules\n\n## Module Job Pipeline\nThe `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js` implements the module lifecycle:\n- `ModuleJob` (line 72) - Main class tracking module loading and dependencies\n- `ModuleJobBase` (line 60) - Base class with common properties\n- `ModuleJobSync` (line 310) - Synchronous variant for require() from ESM\n- The `_link()` method recursively links all dependencies\n- The `run()` method evaluates the module via native ModuleWrap binding\n\n## Native C++ Binding\nThe `src/module_wrap.h` and `src/module_wrap.cc` files contain the `ModuleWrap` class (line 36 in header), which is a V8 Module binding that:\n- Wraps V8's native module implementation\n- Provides `link()` and `evaluate()` methods called by ModuleJob\n- Handles instantiation and evaluation with support for source mapping and debugging\n\n## Supporting Systems\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js` - Default load implementation with `defaultLoad` and `defaultLoadSync`\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/get_format.js` - Format detection via `defaultGetFormat` and MIME type matching\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js` - `Hooks` and `HooksProxy` classes for custom loader registration\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/utils.js` - `compileSourceTextModule`, `initializeESM`, `initializeHooks`\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/initialize_import_meta.js` - `initializeImportMeta` and import.meta.resolve support\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/module_map.js` - `ResolveCache` and `LoadCache` for memoization\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/fetch_module.js` - Remote module fetching for http(s):// URLs\n- `lib/internal/modules/esm/formats.js` - Format constants and detection helpers\n- `src/node_modules.h` - `BindingData` for module resolution internals\n\nThe architecture enables Node.js to transparently load both ESM and CJS modules, with automatic interop bridges allowing require() in CJS to load ESM and import() in ESM to load CJS, supported by a sophisticated caching and memoization layer."
}
